mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in industrial production signals that firms are rebuilding output and inventories, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50% of triggered observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals accelerating household income stress and weaker consumption, causing analysts to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 3-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and CPI is not falling over the prior 3 months, monetary easing works through discount-rate compression without a deflation shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is greater than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-CPI condition has a 60-day mean return less than or equal to the unconditional mean return in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in retail sales above its recent trend signals that household demand is stabilizing, improving revenue expectations for cyclical companies and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses above its 3-month moving average after being below it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales upside crossings are followed by 20-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in more than half of rolling 3-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts from a depressed level signals that construction and housing-linked credit demand have stopped contracting, leading investors to reprice cyclical earnings upward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 600 and crosses above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-level housing-start rebounds do not deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of historical walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly payroll losses indicate collapsing labor income and severe operating leverage pressure on corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the signal is observed.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls by more than 500 over one month
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS monthly declines greater than 500 are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or a negative-hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in real GDP and industrial production identifies broad recession pressure rather than an inventory-only shock, causing persistent earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is negative and INDPRO 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:as of latest snapshot GDPC1=11522.1 and INDPRO=97.3755
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-industrial-production contraction signal has a 60-day return distribution statistically indistinguishable from unconditional returns at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in CPI indicates deflationary demand pressure that raises real debt burdens and reduces nominal revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses below its level from 6 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if CPI six-month declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or by average returns above baseline.
